guideline_references: Australian Rheumatology Association / Therapeutic Guidelines: Rheumatology — acute monoarthritis assessment
eTG complete: The acute hot swollen joint — exclude septic arthritis
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who examined the joint (passive-movement testing, palpation, calf exam) to exclude a septic joint — Breath-Ezy CANNOT examine or aspirate, so an acute hot swollen joint cannot be confidently attributed to a benign flare remotely. The AI Doctor is scored on recognising acute monoarthritis, recognising that septic arthritis cannot be excluded via telehealth, and arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment (not home management).

Anchoring / premature-closure trap: a reassuring background history of self-limiting intermittent joint swelling invites the AI to label this a known inflammatory flare and manage at home, missing the septic-arthritis mimic.
Red-herring: absence of fever does not exclude septic arthritis, and 1st-MTP sparing argues against gout — the case tests whether the AI over-weights a single reassuring feature.
Age (~54) and sex (female) inferred — not stated in the source note (only weight 80 kg and vitals given); flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: AMS (Autoimmune Conditions Mild Severity), source note AMS-032.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
